title: Wollastonia fauriei
taxonomy: Wollastonia fauriei is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Pacific Islands, specifically Hawaii. The plant is classified in the genus Wollastonia, which is part of the tribe Heliantheae. Wollastonia fauriei is closely related to other species in the same genus and is often found in coastal areas. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure.

intro: Wollastonia fauriei, also known as Korean Thistle or Japanese Thistle, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Wollastonia fauriei include flavonoids, saponins, and caffeic acid derivatives, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Korean medicine, Wollastonia fauriei is often prepared as a tea or tincture to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
